body.title: CLOSED: reading arousal from milieu state, not keyword hits
body.result: milieu.read_state() added; book_learner uses it for arousal; cp_affinity in metadata; reading_integrator dead Step 4 retired; 4408 tests pass
body.description:
_arousal_from_cp() computes a keyword-hit score against a 6x7 CP dictionary clamped to [0.10, 0.60] and stamps it as memory.arousal at deposit time. This is a CP-affinity score, not systemic arousal. Fix: at deposit, sample milieu.read_state().arousal (Igor's actual systemic arousal when he read the passage) and write that to memory.arousal. Move the keyword score to metadata['cp_affinity'] (already half-present as identity_weight). Delete _arousal_from_cp() and the duplicate _best_cp/_CP_KEYWORDS_AROUSAL in reading_integrator.py. No models.py touch needed — arousal field exists, we just write the correct value. Prerequisite: confirm milieu.read_state() exists or add it.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/book_learner.py, lab/claudecode/reading_integrator.py, wild_igor/igor/cognition/milieu.py
**Design rules:** none-apply (semantic correction, no schema change)
**Scope boundary:** IN: re-source memory.arousal from milieu; rename keyword score to cp_affinity; delete duplicate keyword dict. OUT: do not change the keyword dictionary data itself; do not change retrieval ranking (downstream reads arousal — verify they handle 0.0-1.0 continuous range).
**Test plan:** test that deposited nodes get arousal from milieu not from keyword count; test that cp_affinity appears in metadata; verify no test hard-codes the old 0.10-0.60 range.
**Pass condition:** A freshly-deposited node has arousal matching milieu state at deposit time, not 0.10-0.60; pytest passes.
